summary: Session 1 features (custom alert message, newest-first history, GET /api/alerts/{id} for sender polling, end-to-end ack flow) fully verified on both backend and frontend mobile UI. 14 new backend tests + 17 existing tests = 31/31 pass. Frontend end-to-end flow on mobile viewport works: login -> tap SEND ALERT -> select Bob -> chip pre-fills input -> custom message overrides -> confirm-send -> alert-sent-screen with waiting-response indicator + ActivityIndicator -> Bob ACKs via API -> sender screen flips to 'They’re OK! Bob Friend responded: I’m OK' within polling window -> Done returns home -> History tab shows alerts sorted newest-first with the custom message text and ACKNOWLEDGED badge; filter-sent/received/missed all work. No JS page errors. Only non-blocking RN-web deprecation warnings (shadow*, pointerEvents, useNativeDriver) observed in console.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~600 lines now (close to 700-line guideline). Route registration order looks correct: /alerts/pending is declared before /alerts/{alert_id}, so FastAPI will still match the literal path first — verified by test_pending_endpoint_not_shadowed.
send-alert.tsx polling loop uses setInterval with 3s tick and re-creates on every alertStatus change — fine for now but could be unified into a single useEffect with refs to reduce timer churn.

Default message fallback in POST /alerts (`'<name> needs you urgently!'`) overrides any empty-string `message` because of `body.message or <default>`. Acceptable, but if the product later wants to allow blank messages explicitly, switch to `if body.message is None`.
Pre-existing console deprecation warnings on web preview: 'shadow*' style props deprecated, props.pointerEvents deprecated, useNativeDriver web fallback. None block functionality.
seed_data_creation: Re-added Bob Friend as Alice’s contact via POST /api/contacts (prior cleanup test had deleted it). Uses existing alice@test.com / bob@test.com seed users + a fresh `carol_<uuid>@test.com` non-participant for 404 isolation test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL=https://alert-bypass.preview.emergentagent.com. New endpoint GET /api/alerts/{alert_id} returns the alert if requester is sender OR receiver, else 404; 401 if no auth. send-alert.tsx polls this endpoint every 3s while sent==true. History sort confirmed via items[].created_at descending. Frontend send-alert flow only renders custom-message-input + chips AFTER a contact is selected (initial screen is contact list). Best path to test Send Alert is to tap text='SEND ALERT' on home, NOT page.goto('/send-alert') (direct deep-link works too but auth context loads later). All required testIDs present and functional: send-alert-screen, select-contact, invite-contact, invite-contact-badge, custom-message-input, quick-msg-{Call me back|Where are you?|I need you now|Emergency!}, confirm-send-alert-button, cancel-alert-button, alert-sent-screen, waiting-response, done-button, history-screen, history-list-item, filter-all/sent/received/missed.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no bugs found. All Session 1 acceptance criteria met: (a) POST /api/alerts accepts optional `message` and persists it (verified via GET /alerts/{id}); when omitted, server defaults to '<sender_name> needs you urgently!'. (b) GET /api/alerts (all/sent/received) is sorted by created_at descending. (c) New GET /api/alerts/{alert_id} returns 200 for sender or receiver, 404 for non-participant, 404 for unknown id, 401 without auth, and does NOT shadow /alerts/pending. (d) Full Bob->Alice alert -> Alice acknowledge -> Bob sees status=acknowledged on GET /alerts/{id} works. (e) Sender UI: TextInput + 4 chips pre-fill, send transitions to alert-sent-screen with ActivityIndicator + 'Waiting for response…'; polling picks up acknowledged within ~3s and UI flips to green check + 'They’re OK! Bob Friend responded: I’m OK'.
